title: Mother, Brother, Another
description: The /ð/ sound in the MIDDLE of words. Dona Lúcia reads a letter. Every 'mother' and 'another' is a chance to practice.
summary_pt_br: O /ð/ no meio das palavras: MOTHER, FATHER, BROTHER, ANOTHER, TOGETHER, WEATHER, RATHER. Praticado com a carta da Dona Lúcia e trechos de músicas do Pink Floyd e Paul Simon.

scenes.subtitle_pt: O zunido no meio das palavras

scenes.text_en:
Dona Lúcia unfolded a letter, old and thin.
Her husband's handwriting, sixty years spun.
'My darling mother,' the letter began,
'I miss my father, my sister, my clan.'
Six /ð/ sounds in two small lines —
the voiced TH echoes across time.
scenes.text_pt:
Dona Lúcia abriu uma carta, fina e antiga.
A letra do marido, sessenta anos de briga.
'My darling mother,' começava a página,
'I miss my father, my sister, my clã.'
Seis sons de /ð/ em duas linhas pequenas —
o TH vibrado atravessa o que se lembra.

scenes.title: TH in the middle — still voiced
scenes.body_en: When TH is between vowels — MOTHER, FATHER, BROTHER, ANOTHER, TOGETHER, WEATHER, RATHER — it's almost always the VOICED /ð/. Same tongue-between-teeth position, same throat vibration.
scenes.body_pt: Quando o TH está NO MEIO, entre vogais, quase sempre é o /ð/ vibrado: MOTHER, FATHER, BROTHER, ANOTHER, TOGETHER, WEATHER. Mesma língua entre os dentes, mesma vibração.

scenes.text_en:
Pink Floyd sang, 'MOTHER, do you think they'll drop the bomb?'
Paul Simon sang, 'Slip out the back, Jack — there must be FIFTY ways.'
Every time a voice says MOTHER, FATHER, BROTHER —
the tongue slips between the teeth, gentle as a feather.
scenes.text_pt:
Pink Floyd cantou 'MOTHER, do you think they'll drop the bomb?' (Mãe, você acha que vão jogar a bomba?)
Paul Simon cantou sobre as 'fifty ways to leave your lover'.
Cada vez que alguém canta MOTHER, FATHER, BROTHER —
a língua escorrega entre os dentes, leve como uma pluma.

exercise.question: Complete: 'My ___ is very kind.' (the parent who gave birth)
exercise.explanation: MOTHER /ˈmʌð.ɚ/ — with /ð/ in the middle. Feel the hum between the vowels.
exercise.hint_pt_br: MOTHER com /ð/ — sinta a vibração entre 'mo-' e '-ther'.

exercise.question: Which of these is pronounced with /ð/ (voiced TH)?
exercise.explanation: BROTHER has /ð/ — between two vowels, voiced. The others are all /θ/ (voiceless).
exercise.hint_pt_br: BROTHER é /ð/. Os outros três (thing, thanks, three) são /θ/.

scenes.location_en: Dona Lúcia's table, the letter open
scenes.location_pt: Na mesa da Dona Lúcia, a carta aberta

turns.en: No — that is my mother-in-law. My husband's mother.
turns.pt: Não — essa é minha sogra. A mãe do meu marido.

turns.en: And who is the other person?
That is my brother. My brother and I — together always.
turns.pt: Esse é meu irmão. Meu irmão e eu — sempre juntos.

exercise.question: Complete: 'Can I have ___ coffee, please?' (= one more coffee)
exercise.explanation: ANOTHER /əˈnʌð.ɚ/ = an + other = 'um outro'. Very common for asking for one more.
exercise.hint_pt_br: ANOTHER = an + other = 'outro / mais um'. Útil para pedir mais um café, mais um taco.

exercise.explanation: Five words that share the middle /ð/. Say them in a chain: 'My mother, father, brother — together — want another.'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco palavras com /ð/ no meio. Frase de treino: 'My mother, father, brother — together — want another.'

scenes.text_en:
Dona Lúcia folded the letter. She smiled.
'Two sounds,' she said. 'One with air, one with hum.
They filled my husband's English — so they'll fill mine too.
I'll write him back. It's something I can do.'
scenes.text_pt:
Dona Lúcia dobrou a carta. Sorriu.
'Dois sons,' ela disse. 'Um de ar, outro de zunido.
Enchiam o inglês do meu marido — e vão encher o meu também.
Vou responder a ele. É algo que eu posso, meu bem.'

scenes.title: Family reunion (TH sound)
scenes.pt_summary: TH sonoro em mother, brother, another, other, together.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Here we are with my whole family.
turns.pt: Aqui estou com minha família inteira.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: My mother is the woman with the red dress.
turns.pt: Minha mãe é a mulher de vestido vermelho.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: My brother is the tall boy on the left.
turns.pt: Meu irmão é o menino alto à esquerda.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Yes, another sister — she is away at college.
turns.pt: Sim, outra irmã — está na faculdade.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: You and your brother look alike.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: People say that all the time.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Can we take a photo together?

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Mother, Brother, Another". The /ð/ sound in the MIDDLE of words. Dona Lúcia reads a letter. Every 'mother' and 'another' is a chance to practice. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Mother, Brother, Another". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Mother, Brother, Another'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Mother, Brother, Another".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias em inglês lento — ótimo para ouvido iniciante.

sources.label: English Grammar In Use (preview)
